meta.certificationName: Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300)
meta.title: MS-PBI-7: Data Preparation, Modeling, Analysis, and Managed Delivery

questions.stem:
A Power BI Desktop file imports data from separate development and production SQL Server databases. Several Power Query source steps need the same server and database values, and the analyst wants to switch environments before publishing without editing each query step.

Which Power Query feature should the analyst use?

options.text: Create Power Query parameters for the server and database values and reference them in the source steps.
options.explanation: Power Query parameters store reusable values that can be referenced by data source functions and transformed query steps, so changing the parameter value changes each source step that uses it. [Power Query parameters](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/power-query-query-parameters)

options.text: Change the data source credentials in Power BI Desktop
options.explanation: Data source credentials control authentication to a source. They do not replace server or database names embedded in multiple Power Query M source expressions.

options.text: Duplicate every query for the production database
options.explanation: Duplicating each query for production would create separate M logic to maintain. It does not centralize the environment-specific connection values.

options.text: Create a report slicer for environment selection
options.explanation: A report slicer filters data after it is loaded into the report. It cannot change Power Query source paths or connection arguments during refresh.

questions.reference: [Power Query parameters for switching data source values during refresh](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/power-query-query-parameters)

questions.stem:
A sales model has one Date table. The relationship from Date[Date] to Sales[OrderDate] is active, and the relationship from Date[Date] to Sales[ShipDate] is inactive. A shipment analysis page needs only the shipped-sales measure to use ShipDate while existing order-date measures keep their current behavior.

Which DAX approach should the model author use?

options.text: Create a calculated column that copies Sales[ShipDate] into the Date table
options.explanation: Copying fact-level shipment dates into the Date table would break the Date table's role as a unique date dimension and would not activate the inactive model relationship for one measure.

options.text: Set cross-filter direction to Both on the OrderDate relationship
options.explanation: Bidirectional filtering changes how filters flow through the active OrderDate relationship. It does not make the measure evaluate through the inactive ShipDate relationship.

options.text: Make both Date relationships active
options.explanation: Making both date relationships active would introduce ambiguous filter propagation between the same Date and Sales tables. Power BI keeps one active path for that relationship pattern.

options.text: Create a measure that uses CALCULATE with USERELATIONSHIP between Date[Date] and Sales[ShipDate].
options.explanation: `USERELATIONSHIP` activates an existing relationship for the duration of a specific calculation, and it is commonly used inside `CALCULATE` to override the active relationship for one measure such as shipped sales by ShipDate. [USERELATIONSHIP function](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dax/userelationship-function-dax)

questions.reference: [USERELATIONSHIP for measures over an inactive role-playing date relationship](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dax/userelationship-function-dax)

questions.stem:
A three-page sales report has a Region slicer on the overview page. Executives want the selected region to filter all report pages, but they only want the slicer control to remain visible on the overview page.

What should the report author configure?

options.text: Create a drillthrough page for Region
options.explanation: Drillthrough passes a filter context when a user navigates to a detail page. It does not maintain one live slicer selection across several report pages.

options.text: Create bookmarks for every region
options.explanation: Bookmarks capture saved report states. Maintaining a bookmark for every region would not provide a dynamic synced slicer selection across pages.

options.text: Use the Sync slicers pane to synchronize the Region slicer across the report pages and control where it is visible.
options.explanation: The Sync slicers pane lets a slicer selection apply to selected report pages and separately controls which pages display the slicer, so the Region filter can affect hidden synced pages. [Sync and use slicers on other pages](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/visuals/power-bi-visualization-slicers#sync-and-use-slicers-on-other-pages)

options.text: Configure Edit interactions on each visual
options.explanation: Edit interactions controls how a selection affects other visuals on the same report page. It does not synchronize slicer state across multiple pages.

questions.reference: [Sync slicers to keep a filter selection consistent across report pages](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/visuals/power-bi-visualization-slicers#sync-and-use-slicers-on-other-pages)

questions.stem:
A Power BI model imports a raw Orders CSV once, applies staging transformations, and then needs separate cleaned Orders, Returns, and Customers queries based on that staged logic. The raw staging query should not appear as an extra table in the model.

Which TWO actions should the analyst take? (Select TWO)

options.text: Create downstream queries as references to the staged Orders query.
options.explanation: Creating downstream queries as references makes each final query depend on the staged Orders query, so shared transformation steps remain centralized instead of being copied into separate query definitions. [Use Power Query to transform data](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/power-query-ui)

options.text: Hide the staged Orders table in report view
options.explanation: Hiding a loaded table only removes it from the report field list. The table still loads into the model, consumes storage, and participates in refresh.

options.text: Clear Enable load for the staged Orders query so only final shaped tables load to the model.
options.explanation: Clearing Enable load on the staging query keeps that intermediate query from loading as its own model table while still allowing referenced downstream queries to use its transformation results. [Use Power Query to transform data](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/power-query-ui)

options.text: Duplicate the staged Orders query for each final table
options.explanation: Duplicating the staged query creates independent copies of the M steps. Later fixes to the staging logic would have to be repeated in each duplicate query.

questions.reference: [Referencing queries and disabling load in Power Query](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/power-query-ui)

questions.stem:
A report visual displays MonthName values alphabetically, such as April before February. The model already contains a MonthNumber column with values 1 through 12, and visuals should still display the friendly month names.

What should the model author configure?

options.text: Create a measure that returns the month number
options.explanation: A measure returns values at query time. It does not define the model-level categorical ordering of a text column on axes and slicers.

options.text: Manually sort each visual by MonthName descending
options.explanation: Sorting MonthName descending still uses text order, so it does not produce January-to-December calendar order and would need to be repeated per visual.

options.text: Prefix the month names with numbers in Power Query
options.explanation: Prefixing month names with numbers changes the business label shown to users. The existing MonthNumber column can define sort order without altering the displayed text.

options.text: Set MonthName to Sort by column using MonthNumber in the model.
options.explanation: Sort by column lets one column define the display order of another column; Microsoft documents month names sorted chronologically by using a month number column. [Sort one column by another column](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/desktop-sort-by-column)

questions.reference: [Sort a text month column by a numeric month index column](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/desktop-sort-by-column)

questions.stem:
The finance team needs a Power BI artifact that exports thousands of invoice pages to PDF. Each invoice must have repeated headers, controlled page breaks, and a fixed print layout rather than an exploratory report canvas.

Which artifact should the team create?

options.text: Use a mobile layout for the report
options.explanation: Mobile layout optimizes how report visuals appear on a phone. It does not create a pixel-perfect printed document with repeated page elements.

options.text: Use a standard interactive report page with a table visual
options.explanation: An interactive report page is optimized for exploration and visual interaction. It is not the right artifact for exact page-oriented invoice output across thousands of pages.

options.text: Create a dashboard with pinned invoice tiles
options.explanation: A dashboard is a single-page monitoring surface made from pinned tiles. It does not provide fixed multi-page layout, repeated headers, or detailed PDF pagination for invoices.

options.text: Create a paginated report in Power BI Report Builder for the invoice layout.
options.explanation: Paginated reports are designed for printing and sharing, can span many pages, and let authors control the report page layout exactly in Power BI Report Builder. [Paginated reports in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/paginated-reports/paginated-reports-report-builder-power-bi)

questions.reference: [Choose a paginated report for pixel-perfect printable invoices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/paginated-reports/paginated-reports-report-builder-power-bi)

questions.stem:
A CSV import has two million customer rows. The first 1,000 preview rows look valid, but rare invalid postal codes might appear later in the file. The analyst needs to assess data quality before loading the model.

What should the analyst do in Power Query Editor?

options.text: Remove duplicate customer rows
options.explanation: Removing duplicates addresses repeated records. It does not reveal rare invalid postal code values that occur outside the profiled sample.

options.text: Create a report-level filter for blank postal codes
options.explanation: A report-level filter is applied after data is loaded into the model. It does not improve Power Query's profiling coverage during data preparation.

options.text: Change column profiling to use the entire dataset before reviewing column quality and distribution results.
options.explanation: Power Query profiles the first 1,000 rows by default, and the profiling scope can be changed to the entire data set so column quality and distribution can detect issues outside the preview sample. [Data profiling tools](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/data-profiling-tools)

options.text: Set the PostalCode data type to Text
options.explanation: Setting PostalCode to Text can preserve formatting such as leading zeros, but it does not make column profiling inspect rows beyond the default preview sample.

questions.reference: [Use Power Query column profiling based on the entire dataset](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/data-profiling-tools)

questions.stem:
A matrix visual that uses a Margin Variance measure takes 12 seconds to render. The model author needs evidence about whether the delay comes from visual rendering or the DAX query, and then needs to inspect or test the generated query.

Which TWO steps should the author use? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use DAX query view to run and refine the captured DAX query while testing the measure logic.
options.explanation: DAX query view can run DAX queries, and visual DAX queries from Performance Analyzer can be opened in DAX query view as a new query tab for testing and modification. [DAX Query View](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/transform-model/dax-query-view)

options.text: Use Performance Analyzer to record the matrix visual timings and copy the generated query.
options.explanation: Performance Analyzer records visual activity in Power BI Desktop and exposes details such as visual display, DAX query, and other timing categories, along with options to copy the generated query. [Performance Analyzer](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/desktop-performance-analyzer)

options.text: Review the gateway timeout settings and capacity memory metrics for the dataset
options.explanation: Gateway timeout settings and capacity memory metrics can be relevant for refresh or service-capacity issues, but they do not show the Power BI Desktop visual timing breakdown or expose the generated DAX query for this matrix visual.

options.text: Create a quick measure to replace the existing Margin Variance measure
options.explanation: A quick measure generates a new DAX pattern. It does not diagnose the existing matrix visual's timings or inspect the query currently produced by the Margin Variance measure.

questions.reference: [Use Performance Analyzer and DAX query view to investigate a slow visual measure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/desktop-performance-analyzer)

questions.stem:
A Power BI report page uses DirectQuery against an operations database. A wallboard must update incident counts every minute while the report is open, without waiting for a scheduled semantic model refresh or requiring users to interact.

What should the report author configure?

options.text: Add a relative time slicer set to the last minute
options.explanation: A relative time slicer changes the filter window shown in the report. It does not trigger periodic visual queries against the DirectQuery source.

options.text: Configure automatic page refresh on the report page with a one-minute refresh interval.
options.explanation: Automatic page refresh lets an active report page query supported DirectQuery sources at a predefined cadence, and a fixed interval refresh sends update queries for all visuals on the page at the configured interval. [Automatic page refresh](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/desktop-automatic-page-refresh)

options.text: Configure daily scheduled refresh for the semantic model
options.explanation: Scheduled refresh updates imported data in a semantic model on a service schedule. It does not make an open DirectQuery report page re-query its visuals every minute.

options.text: Tell users to refresh the browser tab
options.explanation: Refreshing the browser tab is a manual user action. It does not provide an unattended refresh cadence for a wallboard.

questions.reference: [Automatic page refresh for a DirectQuery monitoring report page](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/desktop-automatic-page-refresh)

questions.stem:
A sales operations manager wants an email notification when a Power BI service dashboard card tile for Open Escalations rises above 50. The notification must be based on the numeric tile value crossing a threshold.

What should be configured?

options.text: Add a row-level security role for the manager
options.explanation: Row-level security changes which rows a viewer can see. It does not send threshold-based notifications from dashboard tiles.

options.text: Increase the semantic model refresh frequency
options.explanation: Increasing semantic model refresh frequency can update the tile more often, but it does not define an alert rule or send the manager an email when the value exceeds 50.

options.text: Create a data alert on the numeric dashboard tile and set the threshold above 50.
options.explanation: Power BI data alerts notify users when data in dashboard tiles goes beyond configured limits, and alerts can be set on card, KPI, and gauge tiles with numeric values. [Data alerts in the Power BI service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/service-set-data-alerts)

options.text: Create a report subscription for the page
options.explanation: A report subscription sends a scheduled snapshot of report content. It is not triggered by a dashboard tile value crossing a numeric threshold.

questions.reference: [Configure a Power BI data alert on a dashboard tile threshold](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/service-set-data-alerts)

questions.stem:
A Fabric workspace stores sales fact tables as Delta tables in OneLake. Analysts need Power BI reports to query those lakehouse tables with near-real-time access, and the team wants to avoid copied Import storage and DirectQuery translation through a SQL analytics endpoint.

Which semantic model storage mode should the team use?

options.text: Use Direct Lake storage mode for the semantic model tables backed by the Fabric lakehouse.
options.explanation: Direct Lake is a Power BI semantic model table storage mode in Microsoft Fabric that loads data from Delta tables in OneLake and avoids copying the full data into Import storage or federating queries through DirectQuery. [Direct Lake overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/fundamentals/direct-lake-overview)

options.text: Create a Power Query parameter for the lakehouse path
options.explanation: A Power Query parameter can change connection values used by queries, but it does not set the semantic model table storage mode or provide Direct Lake behavior.

options.text: Use Import mode with a 15-minute scheduled refresh
options.explanation: Import mode creates a cached copy of the data in the semantic model and depends on refresh operations, so it does not meet the requirement to avoid copied storage and refresh cycles.

options.text: Use DirectQuery against the SQL analytics endpoint
options.explanation: DirectQuery federates translated queries to the underlying SQL endpoint. That is different from Direct Lake, which is designed to access supported OneLake Delta data directly.

questions.reference: [Choose Direct Lake for a Fabric lakehouse semantic model without import refresh](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/fundamentals/direct-lake-overview)

questions.stem:
A Product table has a numeric `ProductCode` column. Report authors need to use the code as an identifier in tables and slicers, but Power BI keeps summing it when they drag the field into visuals. The column should remain numeric for relationships and sorting.

Which model setting should the author change?

options.text: Hide the ProductCode column from report view
options.explanation: Hiding the column from report view prevents authors from using the identifier. It does not let them display the numeric code without aggregation.

options.text: Change ProductCode to a decimal number
options.explanation: Changing the column to a decimal number keeps it numeric and still allows Power BI to aggregate it. It does not identify the field as a non-summarized identifier.

options.text: Create a SUM(ProductCode) measure
options.explanation: Creating a `SUM(ProductCode)` measure explicitly defines the unwanted aggregation and does not change how the original column behaves when authors use it in visuals.

options.text: Set the ProductCode column's default summarization to Do not summarize in the model.
options.explanation: `Do Not Summarize` tells Power BI to treat each value separately instead of aggregating the numeric field, which is the intended setting for a numeric ID column that should not be summed. [Work with aggregates in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/service-aggregates)

questions.reference: [Set default summarization to Do not summarize for numeric identifier columns](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/service-aggregates)

questions.stem:
A report page contains two overlapping KPI visuals for compact and expanded views. The designer needs to keep both visuals on the same page, choose which visual is on top, and hide one visual for a bookmark state.

Which Power BI pane should the designer use?

options.text: Create a drillthrough target page
options.explanation: A drillthrough target page navigates users to filtered detail content. It does not control visibility or z-order for visuals that remain on the current page.

options.text: Open the Selection pane and use it to hide or show visuals and reorder their layering.
options.explanation: The Selection pane includes layer-order controls and show or hide controls for report objects, so it can manage both the z-order and visibility of overlapping visuals on the page. [Selection pane navigation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/desktop-accessibility-creating-tools)

options.text: Use Edit interactions for the KPI visuals
options.explanation: Edit interactions changes whether one visual filters or highlights another visual. It does not hide report objects or change which object appears in front.

options.text: Use the Sync slicers pane
options.explanation: The Sync slicers pane shares slicer selections across report pages. It does not manage object visibility or layer order for overlapping visuals.

questions.stem:
A Power BI workspace contains finance reports. The finance leadership group must open and interact with workspace content, but the group must not publish, edit, delete, or manage workspace items.

Which workspace role provides the least privilege for this requirement?

options.text: Assign the Contributor role
options.explanation: The Contributor role can create, edit, and delete workspace content, which exceeds the read-only consumption requirement.

options.text: Assign the finance leadership group the Viewer role in the workspace.
options.explanation: The Viewer role is the least-privileged workspace role for users who need to view and interact with workspace content without creating, editing, deleting, publishing, or managing permissions. [Roles in workspaces in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-roles-new-workspaces)

options.text: Assign the Admin role
options.explanation: The Admin role can update and delete the workspace and manage workspace access, which is far beyond read-only report consumption.

options.text: Assign the Member role
options.explanation: The Member role can add users with lower permissions and publish or update apps, which grants more workspace control than finance leadership needs.

questions.reference: [Assign the Viewer workspace role for read-only workspace consumption](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-roles-new-workspaces)

questions.stem:
A Power Query model must combine customer records from a CRM export and a billing export. The matching company names are text values that differ slightly, such as `Contoso Ltd` and `Contoso Limited`, and the analyst needs controls for similarity and transformations during the match.

Which merge approach should the analyst use?

options.text: Use Merge Queries with fuzzy matching enabled for the company-name columns.
options.explanation: Fuzzy merge applies fuzzy matching algorithms during Power Query merge operations over text columns, allowing approximate matches and options such as similarity threshold and transformation tables. [Fuzzy merge](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/merge-queries-fuzzy-match)

options.text: Change both company-name columns to Whole Number
options.explanation: Changing company names to Whole Number would create conversion errors or meaningless numeric values. Fuzzy matching requires text columns, not numeric conversion.

options.text: Use a standard merge with exact matching only
options.explanation: A standard merge with exact matching only joins rows whose key values match exactly, so it would miss near-duplicate names such as abbreviations or spelling variants.

options.text: Use Append Queries on the two exports
options.explanation: Append Queries stacks rows from one table under another. It does not match related customer records from two tables by company name.

questions.reference: [Use fuzzy matching when merging Power Query tables with inconsistent text keys](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/merge-queries-fuzzy-match)

questions.stem:
An inventory fact table stores daily `OnHandQuantity` snapshots. A monthly report must show the quantity from the last date in each month while still aggregating that final-date quantity across products.

Which DAX approach should the model author use?

options.text: Create a calculated column that stores the month number
options.explanation: A calculated column that stores a month number only labels rows by month. It does not change the aggregation logic so the measure returns the last snapshot in the period.

options.text: Set the Date relationship to bidirectional
options.explanation: Bidirectional filtering changes how filters propagate across relationships. It does not define a semi-additive calculation that evaluates inventory at the final date in the current period.

options.text: Create a DAX measure that evaluates OnHandQuantity at the last date in the current period, such as with CLOSINGBALANCEMONTH or LASTDATE inside CALCULATE.
options.explanation: `CLOSINGBALANCEMONTH` evaluates an expression at the last date of the month in the current context, which fits a period-ending inventory balance measure for daily snapshot data. [CLOSINGBALANCEMONTH function](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dax/closingbalancemonth-function-dax)

options.text: Use SUM(OnHandQuantity) over all dates in the month
options.explanation: Summing `OnHandQuantity` across every day in the month repeatedly counts the same stock position over time and overstates the period-ending balance.

questions.reference: [Create a semi-additive inventory measure that returns period-ending balance](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dax/closingbalancemonth-function-dax)

questions.stem:
A sales report consumer wants to explore a published report by replacing Region with Segment in one chart. The consumer should not edit the semantic model or save a changed report design for everyone.

Which report feature supports this self-service exploration?

options.text: Enable Personalize visuals for the report so consumers can adjust fields or visualization type for their own exploration.
options.explanation: Personalize visuals lets report consumers make their own ad hoc changes to a visual, such as swapping a measure or dimension or changing the visualization type, without requiring edit permission for the report. [Personalize visuals in a report](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/explore-reports/end-user-personalize-visuals)

options.text: Create a bookmark for every Segment value
options.explanation: Bookmarks capture predefined report states. They do not let consumers freely replace the fields used by a visual during their own exploration.

options.text: Add a report page tooltip to the chart
options.explanation: A report page tooltip provides extra hover context for a visual. It does not let a consumer swap fields or change the visualization type.

options.text: Grant every consumer Contributor access to the workspace
options.explanation: Contributor access allows users to edit workspace content, which over-grants permissions when the need is only per-user visual personalization.

questions.reference: [Enable personalized visuals so consumers can change fields or visual type](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/consumer/end-user-personalize-visuals)

questions.stem:
A Power Query `Orders` table must produce one row per `CustomerID`. The result needs a total revenue value for each customer and a nested table containing that customer's original order rows for later expansion.

Which TWO Group By configurations should the analyst use? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use Remove Duplicates on CustomerID
options.explanation: Remove Duplicates on `CustomerID` keeps one row for each customer but does not calculate total revenue or preserve all order rows in a nested table.

options.text: Use Group By on CustomerID and add a Sum aggregation for the revenue column.
options.explanation: A Group By column operation such as Sum can aggregate a numeric column, so grouping by `CustomerID` and summing revenue creates the total revenue value for each customer. [Power Query Group By](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/group-by)

options.text: Use Pivot Column on CustomerID
options.explanation: Pivot Column turns values from a selected column into separate columns. It does not create one grouped customer row with both a revenue aggregation and nested detail rows.

options.text: Add an All Rows aggregation in the same Group By operation to keep each customer's original order rows as a nested table.
options.explanation: The All rows row operation outputs all grouped rows as a table value, which preserves each customer's original order records as a nested table in the grouped result. [Power Query Group By](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/group-by)

questions.stem:
A workspace owner adds a new regional scorecard report to a workspace that is distributed through a Power BI app. App users do not see the new report, and the owner wants consumers to receive it through the existing app rather than being added directly to the workspace.

What should the owner do?

options.text: Grant the consumers Contributor access to the workspace
options.explanation: Granting consumers Contributor access gives workspace edit permissions and bypasses the app distribution model, which over-grants access for report consumption.

options.text: Refresh the semantic model behind the scorecard
options.explanation: Refreshing the semantic model updates data values in existing content. It does not add a newly created report to the published app.

options.text: Update the Power BI app and include the new scorecard report in the appropriate audience.
options.explanation: When updating a Power BI app, newly added workspace content is not included by default, so the owner must update the app and add the new report to the appropriate audience content. [Publish an app in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-create-distribute-apps#update-an-app)

options.text: Create a data alert on the scorecard tile
options.explanation: A data alert notifies users when a numeric dashboard tile crosses a threshold. It does not publish or expose new app content.

questions.stem:
A Power BI report must be reviewed by keyboard and screen reader users before publication to executives. The author needs to improve nonvisual descriptions and the order in which users move through visuals on the report page.

Which TWO accessibility configurations should the author apply? (Select TWO)

options.text: Configure tab order so keyboard navigation follows the intended reading sequence.
options.explanation: Setting tab order lets keyboard users navigate report visuals in an order that matches the intended visual reading sequence, and it can remove decorative objects from that sequence. [Design Power BI reports for accessibility](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/desktop-accessibility-creating-reports)

options.text: Create bookmarks for each executive topic
options.explanation: Bookmarks save report states and can support navigation patterns, but they do not by themselves create alt text for assistive technology or define the tab order of page objects.

options.text: Add conditional formatting to color high and low values
options.explanation: Conditional formatting can visually emphasize high and low values, but color formatting does not provide screen reader descriptions or define keyboard navigation order.

options.text: Add meaningful alt text to important visuals so screen readers can describe their purpose.
options.explanation: Alt text provides an alternative text description for visuals and images so screen reader users can understand the purpose or insight of meaningful report objects. [Design Power BI reports for accessibility](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/desktop-accessibility-creating-reports)

questions.stem:
A Power BI analyst imports a CSV file and changes `InvoiceDate` to the Date data type in Power Query. A few source rows now show conversion errors, and the analyst must inspect the original failed rows before deciding whether to fix or remove them.

Which Power Query action should the analyst use?

options.text: Change the column format in the report visual
options.explanation: Changing a field's report visual format only affects how loaded values are displayed. It does not expose or repair Power Query type-conversion errors created during import.

options.text: Remove duplicate rows from the query
options.explanation: Removing duplicate rows compares row values for duplication. It does not identify which `InvoiceDate` values failed conversion to the Date data type.

options.text: Use Replace Errors to set all failed dates to null
options.explanation: `Replace Errors` substitutes a chosen value for each error. That can be useful after review, but it would hide the original invalid date values before the analyst has inspected the failed rows.

options.text: Use Keep Errors on the InvoiceDate column to return only the rows that failed conversion.
options.explanation: `Keep Errors` filters the query to the rows that contain errors in the selected column, which lets the analyst audit the invalid date values before applying a cleanup decision. [Dealing with errors in Power Query](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/dealing-with-errors)

questions.stem:
A report author has a matrix that already shows monthly sales. The author needs a running total that is calculated only within that matrix layout and should not become a reusable measure in the semantic model.

Which TWO actions should the author take? (Select TWO)

options.text: Add an index column in Power Query
options.explanation: An index column in Power Query is created before report filters, slicers, and visual axes are applied. It cannot produce a running total that follows the current matrix layout.

options.text: Create a calculated column in the fact table
options.explanation: A calculated column is materialized during model processing and stores row-level results. It does not evaluate over the matrix result set or recalculate based on the visual's axis layout.

options.text: Use a visual calculation expression such as a running-sum pattern over the visual axis values.
options.explanation: Visual calculations support expressions such as `RUNNINGSUM([Sales Amount])` that operate over the visual's axis values, which matches a running total within the matrix result set. [Visual calculations overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-visual-calculations-overview)

options.text: Add a visual calculation to the matrix visual.
options.explanation: A visual calculation is created on a specific visual and evaluates over the data already present in that visual, so it keeps the running total scoped to the matrix instead of adding model-wide DAX. [Visual calculations overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-visual-calculations-overview)

questions.stem:
A new Power BI report author needs a year-over-year percentage change calculation quickly. The author wants Power BI Desktop to generate a starting DAX pattern, but the result still must be reviewed against the model's date table and the business definition.

Which approach should the author use?

options.text: Create a calculated column for year-over-year percentage change
options.explanation: A calculated column is evaluated at refresh time for each row. A year-over-year percentage measure must respond to filter context, date context, and report selections.

options.text: Format the sales measure as a percentage
options.explanation: Formatting a sales measure as a percentage only changes how a numeric value is displayed. It does not calculate a prior-year comparison or percentage change.

options.text: Use Auto date/time as the permanent date model
options.explanation: Auto date/time can create hidden date tables for convenience, but it is not a substitute for validating time-intelligence logic against the intended date table and business calendar.

options.text: Create a quick measure for year-over-year change, then inspect and adjust the generated DAX as needed.
options.explanation: Quick measures generate common DAX calculations from choices made in a dialog, and Power BI shows the generated DAX so the author can review, learn from, and adjust it for the model. [Use quick measures for common calculations](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-quick-measures)

questions.stem:
A Power Query source returns JSON where each customer record contains a nested `orders` list. The final table must have one row per order, with the customer attributes repeated on each order row.

Which TWO Power Query actions are needed? (Select TWO)

options.text: Set the column data category to Web URL
options.explanation: Setting a data category such as Web URL changes model metadata used by visuals. It does not parse JSON text or expand nested lists and records in Power Query.

options.text: Use the JSON connector or parse step so Power Query represents the source as records and lists.
options.explanation: Using the JSON connector or `Json.Document` parsing step makes Power Query treat the source as structured JSON values such as records and lists, which is required before nested fields can be flattened. [Power Query JSON connector](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/connectors/json)

options.text: Expand the nested `orders` list to new rows and expand record fields into columns.
options.explanation: Expanding the nested list creates rows for the order items, and expanding record fields creates columns for customer and order attributes, producing the flat table shape required for analysis. [Power Query JSON connector](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/connectors/json)

options.text: Append the JSON table to the customer dimension
options.explanation: Appending tables stacks rows from compatible tables. It does not flatten a nested `orders` list or repeat customer fields for each order record.

questions.stem:
A sales manager wants a Power BI regional scorecard report page delivered by email every Monday morning. The email should be sent on that schedule even when no metric crosses a threshold.

Which Power BI feature should be configured?

options.text: Create a subscription for the report page and set the weekly email schedule.
options.explanation: Power BI report subscriptions send an email containing a snapshot, link, or attachment on a schedule that the subscriber sets, which matches a fixed Monday delivery requirement. [Email subscriptions for reports and dashboards](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/collaborate-share/end-user-subscribe)

options.text: Add the report to a deployment pipeline
options.explanation: Deployment pipelines move content between development, test, and production stages. They do not create scheduled email delivery for report consumers.

options.text: Create a dashboard data alert on a KPI tile
options.explanation: A dashboard data alert is triggered by a tile value meeting a configured threshold. It does not send a report snapshot every Monday regardless of metric values.

options.text: Configure scheduled refresh for the semantic model
options.explanation: Scheduled refresh updates imported data in a semantic model. It does not email a report page snapshot to users on a weekly schedule.

questions.stem:
A Power BI report page works well on desktop, but phone users need a rearranged single-column view that shows only the most important visuals. The desktop canvas must remain unchanged.

Which authoring feature should the report designer use?

options.text: Change the desktop page size to 9:16
options.explanation: Changing the desktop page size to a phone ratio alters the main report canvas. It does not preserve the existing desktop layout while creating a separate mobile-optimized view.

options.text: Use the Power BI mobile layout view to arrange the selected visuals for phone display.
options.explanation: Power BI mobile layout view provides a phone-optimized canvas where the author can arrange report visuals for portrait phone viewing without changing the original desktop report page. [Create mobile-optimized Power BI reports](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/power-bi-create-mobile-optimized-report-about)

options.text: Create a bookmark that hides desktop-only visuals
options.explanation: Bookmarks capture report state, such as filters, visibility, and navigation. They do not define the dedicated phone canvas that Power BI mobile apps use for optimized portrait layouts.

options.text: Publish a second workspace app for phone users
options.explanation: Publishing a separate workspace app changes how content is distributed. It does not rearrange the visuals on a report page for phone users.

questions.stem:
A Power BI model keeps relationships single-directional for predictable filtering and performance. One DAX measure must count products that had sales in the currently selected date range, requiring filter propagation to behave differently only for that calculation.

Which DAX approach should the model author use?

options.text: Create a calculated column on the product table
options.explanation: A calculated column is computed during refresh and stores a fixed row-level value. It cannot respond to the selected date range in a report visual.

options.text: Turn on Show items with no data in the visual
options.explanation: Show items with no data controls whether categories with blank measure results appear in a visual. It does not change relationship filter direction in DAX.

options.text: Change the relationship to bidirectional in the model
options.explanation: Changing the model relationship to bidirectional changes filter propagation for every calculation and visual that uses that relationship. The requirement is limited to one measure.

options.text: Use CROSSFILTER inside CALCULATE for the measure that needs the temporary filter-direction override.
options.explanation: `CROSSFILTER` specifies the cross-filter direction to use for an existing relationship during a calculation, and it is commonly used inside `CALCULATE` when only one measure needs that relationship behavior. [CROSSFILTER function](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dax/crossfilter-function-dax)

questions.stem:
A finance workspace contains several reports, dashboards, and semantic models. An external auditor should view only one published report for a two-week review and should not receive access to unrelated workspace content.

What should the workspace owner do?

options.text: Assign the auditor the Viewer role on the workspace
options.explanation: The Viewer workspace role grants read access to content in the workspace. That would over-grant access when the auditor should see only a single report.

options.text: Share the specific report or manage its direct access for the auditor with read permission.
options.explanation: Sharing a specific Power BI report or managing direct access grants access to that item without assigning the auditor a workspace role over the rest of the workspace content. [Share Power BI reports and dashboards](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-share-dashboards)

options.text: Publish a new Power BI app containing every finance artifact
options.explanation: A Power BI app packages selected workspace content for distribution. Publishing an app containing every finance artifact would broaden access instead of limiting the auditor to one report.

options.text: Give Build permission on the semantic model only
options.explanation: Build permission on a semantic model lets a user create new content from that model. It does not by itself grant permission to open and view a specific report.

questions.stem:
A CSV file from Germany stores dates such as `31.12.2025` and numbers such as `1.234,56`. Power BI Desktop is using a different local regional setting and interprets the values incorrectly during import.

Which Power Query action should the analyst use?

options.text: Use Change Type with Locale and select the appropriate German locale for the affected columns.
options.explanation: `Change Type` with locale lets the analyst choose both the target data type and the culture used to interpret the source values, so German date and number formats are parsed according to the file's locale. [Data types in Power Query: Using locale](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/data-types#using-locale)

options.text: Replace every period with a slash and every comma with a period
options.explanation: Manual character replacement is fragile because the same punctuation can mean different things in dates and thousands or decimal separators. Locale-aware conversion applies the culture's parsing rules directly.

options.text: Format the date and currency fields in the report canvas
options.explanation: Report canvas formatting happens after the data is imported. It cannot repair dates or numbers that Power Query already parsed incorrectly.

options.text: Set the model language to German in report settings
options.explanation: The model language affects metadata and linguistic behavior in the report experience. It does not control the culture Power Query uses when converting text to dates or numbers.

questions.stem:
An imported sales table stores `OrderTimestamp` down to the second, but every report groups sales only by calendar date. The time-of-day detail has no analytical value and is increasing the number of distinct values stored in the model.

Which modeling change should reduce storage while preserving the reporting requirement?

options.text: Create a visual-level filter that hides time values
options.explanation: A visual-level filter affects query results after the model is loaded. It does not change the encoded values or storage footprint of the imported `OrderTimestamp` column.

options.text: Create an auto date/time hierarchy from the timestamp column and use only the Date level in reports
options.explanation: An auto date/time hierarchy can make report authors group values by date, but it does not remove the original second-level timestamp values from the imported column or reduce that column's storage cardinality.

options.text: Transform OrderTimestamp to a Date column, or split and remove the unused time component before loading the model.
options.explanation: Removing unused detail from imported columns reduces the values the VertiPaq storage engine must encode; when reports only need calendar dates, loading a Date value instead of second-level timestamps lowers unnecessary cardinality. [Data reduction techniques for Import modeling](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/guidance/import-modeling-data-reduction)

options.text: Set the column's default summarization to Do not summarize
options.explanation: Default summarization controls how a field behaves when placed in visuals. It does not remove second-level timestamp values or reduce column cardinality in storage.

questions.stem:
A Power BI analyst receives a product attributes table with columns `ProductID`, `AttributeName`, and `AttributeValue`. Each product has separate rows for Color, Size, and Brand, but the semantic model needs one row per product with those attributes as separate columns.

Which Power Query transformation should the analyst use before creating relationships?

options.text: Use Pivot Column on `AttributeName`, with `AttributeValue` as the values column.
options.explanation: Pivoting `AttributeName` creates one output column for each attribute value, and using `AttributeValue` as the values column fills those new Color, Size, and Brand columns for each product row. [Pivot columns](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/pivot-columns)

options.text: Append the attributes table to the product table
options.explanation: Append combines rows from one table with rows from another table. It does not transform attribute-value rows into separate columns on a single product row.

options.text: Use Unpivot Columns on Color, Size, and Brand
options.explanation: Unpivot Columns converts existing columns into attribute-name rows. In this table, Color, Size, and Brand are values in `AttributeName`, so unpivoting would move in the opposite direction and would not create model-ready columns.

options.text: Create a report-level matrix visual with `AttributeName` on columns
options.explanation: A matrix visual can display `AttributeName` values across columns for one report view, but it does not reshape the Power Query table or create physical columns for relationships in the semantic model.

questions.stem:
A semantic model contains `CustomerKey` and `ProductKey` columns. The columns are required for relationship filtering, but report authors are dragging them into visuals by mistake because they appear in the Fields pane.

What should the modeler do?

options.text: Delete the key columns from the model
options.explanation: Deleting the key columns removes the columns that the relationships depend on, so relationship filtering between the dimension and fact tables would break.

options.text: Change the key columns to Text data type
options.explanation: Changing a key column to Text changes its storage and comparison behavior, but it does not hide the column from the Fields pane or preserve a cleaner reporting surface.

options.text: Set the key columns to Do not summarize
options.explanation: Setting a numeric key to Do not summarize only changes aggregation behavior. The field would still be visible for report authors to place on visuals.

options.text: Set the technical key columns to Hidden in the semantic model.
options.explanation: Hiding relationship key columns keeps them in the model for relationships while removing them from the normal reporting surface, which helps report authors focus on fields intended for visuals. [Power BI optimization guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/guidance/power-bi-optimization)

questions.stem:
A sales director wants to start with total revenue and interactively break it down by Region, Channel, Product Category, and Salesperson. The director also wants Power BI to suggest useful split points while exploring the largest contributors.

Which visual should the report author use?

options.text: Use a Decomposition tree visual with Revenue as Analyze and the breakdown fields in Explain by.
options.explanation: The Decomposition tree visual analyzes a measure across multiple dimensions, lets users drill down in any order through fields in Explain by, and includes AI splits for suggested next dimensions. [Decomposition tree visuals](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/visuals/power-bi-visualization-decomposition-tree)

options.text: Add a Q&A visual and type revenue questions
options.explanation: A Q&A visual answers natural-language questions, but it is not the guided contributor-analysis visual that exposes Analyze, Explain by, and AI split behavior.

options.text: Use a stacked column chart with Region as the legend
options.explanation: A stacked column chart with Region as the legend shows one fixed grouping. It cannot let the user branch through Channel, Product Category, and Salesperson in arbitrary order from the same metric.

options.text: Create a drillthrough page filtered by Salesperson
options.explanation: A drillthrough page sends a user to a predefined detail page filtered by a selected value. It does not provide a single visual where the director can choose different split paths dynamically.

questions.stem:
A certified semantic model is published in a workspace. Analysts in another department need to create their own reports connected to that model, but they should not receive edit access to the source workspace.

Which permission should be granted?

options.text: Grant the analysts Build permission on the semantic model.
options.explanation: Build permission applies to semantic models and allows users to build new content, including reports, on an existing semantic model without requiring workspace edit rights. [Build permission for shared semantic models](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/connect-data/service-datasets-build-permissions)

options.text: Assign the analysts the Viewer role in the source workspace
options.explanation: The Viewer workspace role is intended for consuming workspace content. It is not the least-privilege way to enable creating new reports from a shared semantic model unless Build permission is also provided.

options.text: Share only the existing report with Read permission
options.explanation: Read permission on an existing report allows consumption of that report. It does not by itself grant the ability to create new reports based on the underlying semantic model.

options.text: Give the analysts Admin access to the source workspace
options.explanation: Admin access to the source workspace is far broader than required. It allows workspace management instead of only authoring content from the certified semantic model.

questions.stem:
A fact table has `SourceSystem` and `OrderNumber`, and `OrderNumber` repeats across source systems. A dimension table has the same two columns and must relate uniquely to the fact table after loading.

Which TWO Power Query actions should the analyst take before defining the relationship? (Select TWO)

options.text: Create the same composite key transformation in the dimension query and use it as the one-side relationship key.
options.explanation: Creating the same combined key in the dimension query gives the one-side table a matching key at the same grain, so the relationship can use consistent values from both tables. [Table.CombineColumns](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powerquery-m/table-combinecolumns)

options.text: Relate the tables on `OrderNumber` alone
options.explanation: Relating on `OrderNumber` alone leaves duplicate key values whenever different source systems reuse the same order number, so the relationship cannot distinguish those orders correctly.

options.text: Append the fact and dimension tables before modeling
options.explanation: Appending the fact and dimension tables stacks their rows into one table. It does not create a key that relates two separate tables at the correct grain.

options.text: Merge `SourceSystem` and `OrderNumber` into a single composite key column in the fact query.
options.explanation: Creating a combined `SourceSystem` and `OrderNumber` column in the fact query gives each fact row a relationship key that distinguishes the same order number across different source systems. [Table.CombineColumns](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powerquery-m/table-combinecolumns)

questions.stem:
A disconnected `TargetScenario` table feeds a slicer where users choose a scenario. A measure must read the selected scenario and use `"Base"` when no scenario or multiple scenarios are selected.

Which DAX expression should the measure use to retrieve the scenario value?

options.text: Create a calculated column in the fact table for the selected scenario
options.explanation: A calculated column is evaluated during data refresh, so it cannot respond dynamically to a user's slicer selection in report filter context.

options.text: Use `MAX(TargetScenario[Scenario])` without a fallback
options.explanation: `MAX(TargetScenario[Scenario])` returns one value even when multiple text values are selected, which silently masks ambiguity instead of applying the required fallback.

options.text: Use `VALUES(TargetScenario[Scenario])` directly as the scalar result
options.explanation: `VALUES(TargetScenario[Scenario])` returns a table of distinct values. Used directly where a scalar is required, it can fail or be ambiguous when more than one scenario is in context.

options.text: Use `SELECTEDVALUE(TargetScenario[Scenario], "Base")` inside the measure.
options.explanation: `SELECTEDVALUE` returns the column value only when the filter context has one distinct value; otherwise it returns the alternate result, so `"Base"` handles no selection or multiple selections. [SELECTEDVALUE](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dax/selectedvalue-function-dax)

questions.stem:
A report page shows monthly revenue as a continuous line chart. Stakeholders want Power BI to project the next six months and display a confidence interval from the existing time series, without adding forecast rows to the model.

Which feature should the report author use?

options.text: Use the line chart's Analytics pane to add a Forecast and set the forecast length to six months.
options.explanation: The Analytics pane includes Forecast for line charts, and its settings include forecast length and confidence interval for projecting future values from time data. [Analytics pane forecast](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-analytics-pane)

options.text: Create six blank future rows in Power Query
options.explanation: Adding blank rows in Power Query changes the loaded table but does not create the statistical forecast or visual confidence interval provided by the line chart Analytics pane.

options.text: Enable anomaly detection on the line chart
options.explanation: Anomaly detection identifies unexpected points in existing data. It does not generate future projected values for the next six months.

options.text: Add a constant reference line at the current average revenue
options.explanation: A constant reference line marks a fixed comparison value on a visual. It does not predict future months or calculate a confidence interval.

questions.stem:
An imported semantic model uses SharePoint Online and Azure SQL Database sources. Business users need the Power BI service to refresh the model automatically every weekday morning without someone opening Power BI Desktop.

Which TWO settings must be configured for the semantic model in the Power BI service? (Select TWO)

options.text: Create a report subscription for weekday mornings
options.explanation: A report subscription emails report snapshots to recipients. It does not refresh the imported semantic model data before the subscription runs.

options.text: Configure a dashboard data alert on a KPI tile
options.explanation: A dashboard data alert monitors tile values against a threshold. It is a notification feature and does not schedule semantic model refresh.

options.text: Configure valid data source credentials for the semantic model in the Power BI service.
options.explanation: The semantic model needs valid data source credentials so the Power BI service can sign in to the SharePoint Online and Azure SQL Database sources during refresh. [Configure scheduled refresh](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/connect-data/refresh-scheduled-refresh)

options.text: Enable scheduled refresh and set the weekday refresh time in the semantic model settings.
options.explanation: Scheduled refresh must be enabled and assigned refresh times in the semantic model settings so the service initiates refresh automatically on weekday mornings. [Configure scheduled refresh](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/connect-data/refresh-scheduled-refresh)

questions.stem:
A Power BI analyst imports orders with an `Amount` column. The model needs a reusable `OrderBand` column with High, Medium, or Low labels based on amount thresholds, and the labels must be created during query shaping before load.

Which Power Query feature should the analyst use?

options.text: Use Add Column > Conditional Column in Power Query to create `OrderBand` from threshold rules on `Amount`.
options.explanation: A Conditional Column creates a new column whose values are based on one or more conditions applied to other columns, which fits row-by-row threshold labels during query shaping. [Add a conditional column](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/add-conditional-column)

options.text: Apply conditional formatting to the `Amount` field in a table visual
options.explanation: Conditional formatting changes how values appear in a specific visual. It does not create a reusable `OrderBand` column in the Power Query output.

options.text: Create a dashboard data alert on total order amount
options.explanation: A dashboard data alert monitors a dashboard tile against a threshold. It does not classify each imported order row during query shaping.

options.text: Set the `Amount` column's default summarization to Average
options.explanation: Default summarization controls how a numeric field aggregates in visuals. It does not create threshold-based text labels for rows.

questions.stem:
A product dimension includes a `ThumbnailUrl` column containing HTTPS links to product images. In a table visual, Power BI currently displays the links as text, but the report should render the images while keeping the URL column in the model.

What should the modeler configure?

options.text: Set the `ThumbnailUrl` column's data category to Image URL.
options.explanation: Power BI can display dynamic images from URL values when the column is selected in Table view and its Data category is set to Image URL, then the column is used in a supported visual. [Display images in tables, matrices, and slicers](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/power-bi-images-tables)

options.text: Change the `ThumbnailUrl` data type from Text to Binary
options.explanation: Changing the column to Binary would not use Power BI's URL-based image rendering path. The documented approach keeps URLs in a column and categorizes them as Image URL.

options.text: Encode the images as Base64 strings in Power Query and store them in a Binary column
options.explanation: Power Query can work with binary content, but storing image data in a Binary column is not the documented rendering path for table visuals. Power BI renders supported HTTPS image links from URL text columns categorized as Image URL.

options.text: Set the column's summarization to Do not summarize
options.explanation: Do not summarize affects aggregation behavior for a field. It does not tell table, matrix, slicer, or card visuals to render URL text as images.

questions.stem:
A Power BI analyst imports an Excel sales export into Power Query. The `Region` value appears only on the first customer row for each region, and subtotal or blank rows are mixed between the customer detail rows.

The loaded table must have one row per customer, and every row must have a populated `Region` before the table is related to other model tables. Which TWO shaping steps should the analyst apply? (Select TWO)

options.text: Create a relationship from the blank `Region` rows to a region dimension
options.explanation: A relationship can propagate filters between tables, but it cannot fill missing values in the query result or remove subtotal rows that were loaded from the Excel export.

options.text: Filter out subtotal and blank detail rows before loading the cleaned customer-level table.
options.explanation: Filtering out subtotal and blank detail rows keeps the query output at the required customer grain instead of loading extra rows that would duplicate totals or create noncustomer records in the model. [Fill values in a column](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/fill-values-column)

options.text: Change the `Region` column data category to Place
options.explanation: Setting the `Region` column data category to Place can help map visuals interpret geographic values, but it does not change sparse column values or remove subtotal rows during Power Query shaping.

options.text: Use Fill Down on the `Region` column so each customer row inherits the current region value.
options.explanation: Fill Down replaces null values in a column with the last nonempty value above them, so each customer detail row inherits the appropriate `Region` group label from the source export. [Fill values in a column](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/fill-values-column)

questions.stem:
A report needs to show a product's rank by sales within the currently selected category and date range. The rank must change when report users adjust slicers.

What should the modeler create?

options.text: Sort the visual by the Sales column only
options.explanation: Sorting a visual by Sales changes display order in that one visual, but it does not create a reusable rank measure for labels, filters, or conditional logic.

options.text: Create a DAX measure that uses `RANKX` over the visible product set and the sales measure.
options.explanation: `RANKX` returns the ranking of a value over a table expression, so a measure can evaluate the current visible product set and sales measure at query time as slicers change. [RANKX](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dax/rankx-function-dax)

options.text: Create a calculated column with a static product rank
options.explanation: A calculated column stores its result during model refresh, so the product rank would not recalculate for each category and date slicer selection in the report.

options.text: Set the Product ID column to Do not summarize
options.explanation: Setting Product ID to Do not summarize affects aggregation behavior for that identifier field; it does not compute a dynamic sales rank.

questions.stem:
A line chart shows monthly forecasted demand. The model already includes measures for the upper and lower confidence bounds for each month, and the uncertainty range must appear on the existing chart.

Which report feature should the analyst configure?

options.text: Add a forecast from the Analytics pane
options.explanation: A forecast from the Analytics pane generates projected future values from the visual's existing series; it is not the feature for displaying already prepared upper and lower confidence-bound measures.

options.text: Add a constant reference line at the average demand
options.explanation: A constant reference line can mark one fixed value such as an average, but it cannot show month-specific upper and lower uncertainty ranges around each line-chart point.

options.text: Use a KPI visual with the forecast as the target
options.explanation: A KPI visual compares an indicator to a target, but it does not draw confidence interval bars or bands around each point in the existing line chart.

options.text: Configure error bars on the line chart and bind the upper and lower bound measures.
options.explanation: Power BI error bars can be added to supported visuals from the Analytics pane, and the By field method lets you specify upper and lower bound fields from the semantic model. [Use the Analytics pane in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/transform-model/desktop-analytics-pane)

questions.stem:
A workspace owner plans to rename fields in a certified semantic model. Reports and dashboards in several workspaces are built from that model, and affected owners must be identified before the change is published.

Which TWO actions should the owner take before publishing? (Select TWO)

options.text: Run impact analysis for the semantic model to identify affected reports, dashboards, and contacts.
options.explanation: Semantic model impact analysis is designed to assess downstream reports and dashboards affected by a semantic model change and includes a Notify contacts option for impacted workspaces. [Semantic model impact analysis](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-dataset-impact-analysis)

options.text: Change the endorsement from Certified to Promoted
options.explanation: Changing an endorsement from Certified to Promoted changes the trust label shown to users, but it does not discover dependencies or notify owners of content built on the semantic model.

options.text: Review only the report usage metrics
options.explanation: Usage metrics show consumption activity such as views and visitors, but they do not provide the full dependency path needed to find every report or dashboard that could break after a schema change.

options.text: Open lineage view to trace the semantic model's upstream and downstream dependencies.
options.explanation: Lineage view shows relationships between Power BI artifacts, including upstream and downstream connections for semantic models, reports, dashboards, and other workspace content. [Data lineage](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/collaborate-share/service-data-lineage)

questions.stem:
A semantic model has a calculation group item that can return either margin amount or margin percent. The displayed format must switch between currency and percentage while preserving a numeric value for aggregation, sorting, and conditional formatting.

What should the modeler define?

options.text: Set the measure's data category to Currency
options.explanation: A data category describes how a column should be interpreted, such as geographic or web data; it does not provide context-dependent numeric format strings for a measure.

options.text: Define a dynamic format string expression for the measure or calculation item.
options.explanation: Dynamic format strings let a measure keep its numeric data type while applying different format strings depending on context, and the same DAX patterns also work with calculation groups. [Create dynamic format strings for measures](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/desktop-dynamic-format-strings)

options.text: Create separate text columns for amount and percent labels
options.explanation: Separate text columns can display labels, but they are fixed model columns and do not preserve one context-sensitive numeric measure for visuals.

options.text: Wrap the measure in the DAX `FORMAT` function
options.explanation: The DAX `FORMAT` function returns text, so visuals that need numeric values for charts, sorting, aggregation, or conditional formatting would no longer receive a numeric measure result.

questions.stem:
A Power Query table contains product codes such as `BK-2026-XL`. The analyst needs a new column that contains only the middle year segment for many rows with the same pattern, and the transformation should be generated from examples without manually writing M code.

Which feature should the analyst use?

options.text: Use Group By on the product code column
options.explanation: Group By aggregates rows into summary groups; it does not create a row-by-row text segment extracted from each product code.

options.text: Use Column from Examples and enter sample year values so Power Query infers the extraction logic.
options.explanation: Column from Examples creates a new column from sample output values and can infer the matching transformation for the remaining rows in the Power Query preview. [Add a column from examples](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/column-from-example)

options.text: Set the product code column's data type to Date
options.explanation: Changing the product code data type to Date would try to parse the entire compound code as a date value, not extract the middle year segment.

options.text: Create a DAX calculated column with the extracted year after loading
options.explanation: A DAX calculated column would be created after the data is loaded into the model, but the requirement is to shape the data in Power Query before loading.

questions.stem:
A customer success report must show which account attributes, such as contract length and support plan, most influence whether an account churned. The visual should analyze explanatory fields and rank the factors associated with the churn outcome.

Which visual should the analyst use?

options.text: Use a decomposition tree with churn as the root metric
options.explanation: A decomposition tree is useful for drilling into metric breakdowns across dimensions, but it is not the AI visual that ranks factors associated with a selected outcome.

options.text: Use a waterfall chart by support plan
options.explanation: A waterfall chart explains sequential increases and decreases in a value; it does not analyze multiple explanatory account attributes to identify churn drivers.

options.text: Use the Key influencers visual with churn as the analyzed outcome and account attributes as explanatory fields.
options.explanation: The Key influencers visual analyzes a metric, ranks the factors that matter, and uses Analyze and Explain by fields to show drivers of outcomes such as churn. [Create key influencers visualizations](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/visuals/power-bi-visualization-influencers)

options.text: Use a slicer for each account attribute
options.explanation: Slicers let report users filter by account attributes, but they do not evaluate or rank which attributes influence the churn outcome.

questions.stem:
External consultants can view a shared Power BI report. They are allowed to export chart totals, but they must not download the underlying transaction rows.

Which report setting should the designer use?

options.text: Set the report export data option to allow only summarized data and not underlying data.
options.explanation: Power BI report designers can control export behavior by allowing summarized data only, allowing both summarized and underlying data, or disallowing export, so summarized-only export matches the row-detail restriction. [Export data from a Power BI visualization](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/visuals/power-bi-visualization-export-data)

options.text: Grant Build permission on the semantic model
options.explanation: Build permission lets users create new content from a semantic model and can allow broader model exploration; it is not a setting that limits visual exports to summarized totals.

options.text: Create a dashboard data alert on each sensitive visual
options.explanation: Dashboard data alerts send notifications when numeric thresholds are met, but they do not control whether users can export summarized or underlying data from report visuals.

options.text: Hide the transaction table in report view
options.explanation: Hiding a transaction table reduces clutter for authors in the Fields pane, but hidden fields can still be part of model data and hiding alone does not set export granularity for report consumers.

questions.stem:
A margin variance measure repeats the same filtered prior-period sales expression several times. The formula is hard to maintain, and the repeated expression should be calculated once inside the measure and reused in the final result.

What should the modeler use?

options.text: Move the repeated expression into a calculated column
options.explanation: A calculated column is evaluated during refresh and stores row-level results, so it cannot replace a filter-context-dependent intermediate expression inside a measure.

options.text: Define the repeated expression in a `VAR` and reference it in the measure's `RETURN` expression.
options.explanation: A DAX `VAR` stores the result of an expression as a named variable, and that variable can then be referenced in the measure's `RETURN` expression without recalculating the expression each time it is used. [VAR](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dax/var-dax)

options.text: Set the measure's default summarization to Average
options.explanation: Default summarization applies to columns placed in visuals, not to measure expression evaluation or caching repeated DAX logic.

options.text: Create a Power Query parameter for the prior period
options.explanation: A Power Query parameter is resolved during data preparation and refresh; it does not store or reuse intermediate DAX results at report query time.

questions.stem:
A sales report page needs a generated prose summary that calls out the main changes in revenue and units sold for the currently filtered view. The summary must appear as narrative text on the report page and update with report interactions.

Which visual should the analyst add?

options.text: Add a Power Automate visual to send an approval flow
options.explanation: A Power Automate visual triggers workflows from a report, such as approvals or notifications, but it does not summarize report data in natural language.

options.text: Add a Smart narrative visual to the page and bind it to the relevant report visuals or measures.
options.explanation: The Smart narrative visualization provides text summaries of visuals and reports, can address trends and key takeaways, and updates its generated text and dynamic values when report interactions cross-filter the page. [Create smart narrative summaries](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/visuals/power-bi-visualization-smart-narrative)

options.text: Add a card visual for total revenue
options.explanation: A card visual displays a single metric value, such as total revenue, but it does not generate narrative text about changes across revenue and units sold.

options.text: Add a Q&A visual and type a saved question
options.explanation: A Q&A visual answers user-entered natural-language questions, but it is not designed to persist as an automatically generated prose summary of report trends on the page.
